zh.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold於1444年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾波爾德。他於1464年10月20日與Dulcia Savage結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1497年7月20日在英國藍卡爾波爾德享年53歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾哈爾頓維德斯法爾沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold于1444年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾波爾德。他于1464年10月20日与Dulcia Savage结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1497年7月20日在英国藍卡爾波爾德享年53岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾哈爾頓維德斯法爾沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Staffordshire): from Middle English bold ‘dwelling-house’ (Old English bold the West Midland and northwestern form of Old English bōthl, bōtl), especially in the sense ‘castle or manor house’ for someone who lived or worked at the main house, or a habitational name from one of the many places called with this word, for example Bold (Lancashire), The Bold in Aston Botterell (Shropshire), Booden Farm in Haughton, Booth in Blithfield, and Bull Bridge in Penkridge (all Staffordshire). Compare Bodle .
English: nickname from Middle English bold, Old English b(e)ald ‘bold, brave, noble’, which in Middle English developed the additional sense ‘arrogant, impudent, shameless’.
English: variant of   Bolt   and possibly a variant of   Bald   2.

variants.name: Bollmann
meanings: German:
elaborated derivative of the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo (see Bold 4).

topographic name for a man who lived on a rounded hill, from Middle Low German bol ‘rounded hill’ + man ‘man’, or a habitational name for someone from any of several places called Boll (see Boll ). Compare Bolman .

variants.name: Bowlds
meanings: English: possibly a variant of   Bold  .

variants.name: Boldman
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German Boldemann: from a compound of a variant of the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo (see Bold 4) + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Bodle
meanings: English: habitational name from Buddle Oak in Halse (Somerset), Buddle in Fordingbridge (Hampshire), Buddle in Niton (Isle of Wight), Budleigh in Moretonhampstead (Devon), or Budle in Bamburgh (Northumberland), all named from Middle English bothel, Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’, probably denoting a homestead of some considerable size. See also Bold .
English (West Midlands): variant of   Beadle  .

variants.name: Bolds
meanings: English: variant of Bold , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Boldt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hermann, Kurt, Bernhard, Dieter, Egon, Gerd, Gerhard, Gunter, Gunther, Heinz, Hilde.
German and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, a short form of various compound names with the first element bald ‘bold’. See also German Bold , compare Bolt .

variants.name: Bolda
meanings: Polish: from the German personal name Bold (see Bold 4).
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name Boldizsár, Hungarian form of Baltazar .

variants.name: Bolt
meanings: English: from Middle English bolt ‘bolt, bar’, also ‘bundle’ (Old English bolt ‘arrow’). One of the most common senses of this word was ‘the blunt-headed bolt of a crossbow’, so it could have been given to an archer or to a maker of iron bars or bolts. Bolt was also used metaphorically for a short, thickset, or tubby person and for someone who was physically upright as a bolt, i.e. ‘as straight as an arrow’.
English: variant of   Bold   1.

Dutch, North German, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, a short form of various compound names with the first element bald ‘bold’. See also German Bold , compare German and Danish Boldt .

variants.name: Bolton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in northern England named Bolton (Lancashire, Northumberland, Westmorland, and Yorkshire) or from Boulton in Derbyshire and East Lothian in Scotland, from Old English bothl ‘dwelling, house’ (see Bold 1) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Bolding
meanings: German: patronymic from   Bold   as a personal name.
Danish: habitational name from a place so named in Jutland.
English (Middlesex): variant of   Balding   1.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold was born in 1444, in Bold, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. He married Dulcia Savage on 20 October 1464. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 20 July 1497, in Bold, Lancashire, England, at the age of 53, and was buried in Farnworth, Widnes, Halton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold est né en 1444, à Bold, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Dulcia Savage le 20 octobre 1464. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 20 juillet 1497, à Bold, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Farnworth, Widnes, Halton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold wurde 1444 in Bold, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat am 20. Oktober 1464 Dulcia Savage geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 20. Juli 1497 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Bold, Lancashire, England und wurde in Farnworth, Widnes, Halton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Henry De Boldは1444年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアボルドで生まれた。1464年10月20日にDulcia Savageと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1497年7月20日にイギリスランカシュアイアボルドで53歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスチェシュアイアハルトンウィドンスファルンウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.Farnworth, Widnes, Halton, Cheshire, Eng: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 할턴 위드네스 패른워스
ko.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold(은)는 1444년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 볼드에서 태어났다. 그는 1464년 10월 20일 Dulcia Savage(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1497년 7월 20일 영국 랭카실르 볼드에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 할턴 위드네스 패른워스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold nasceu em 1444, em Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele casou-se com Dulcia Savage em 20 de outubro de 1464. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 20 de julho de 1497, em Bold, Lancashire, Inglaterra, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Farnworth, Widnes, Halton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Farnworth, Widnes, Halton, Cheshire, Eng: фарнворт, виднесь, хэлтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Henry De Bold родился в 1444 г. в местности болд, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. Он женился на женщине по имени Dulcia Savage 20 октября 1464 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 20 июля 1497 г. в местности болд, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности фарнворт, виднесь, хэлтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.
